largest_files.summary: Join prompt values from multiple queued commands into one. Strings are newline-joined; if any value is a block array, all values are normalized to blocks and concatenated.
Appends a memory correction hint to a rejection/cancellation message when auto-memory is enabled and the GrowthBook flag is on.
Transcript persistence backbone. Writes JSONL session history, file snapshots, metadata, and sidecar task state so sessions can resume and background work can be recovered.
Hooks are user-defined shell commands that can be executed at various points in Claude Code's lifecycle.
Interactive terminal runtime. Owns the message list, prompt UX, hooks, permissions UI, background tasks, bridge state, MCP state, and the main user-facing agent loop.
Primary CLI bootstrap. Starts early prefetch work, wires feature-gated modules, initializes plugins and skills, parses the command line, and launches the interactive runtime.

Pure-TypeScript bash parser producing tree-sitter-bash-compatible ASTs. Downstream code in parser.ts, ast.ts, prefix.ts, ParsedCommand.ts walks this by field name. startIndex/endIndex are UTF-8 BYTE offsets (not JS string
Whether the file was truncated due to size limits
Assemble the extra body parameters for the API request, based on the CLAUDE_CODE_EXTRA_BODY environment variable if present and on any beta headers (primarily for Bedrock requests).
MCP client runtime. Connects to MCP servers, exposes tools/resources/commands, and handles authentication, transport normalization, and error shaping.
Plugin discovery and loading pipeline. Resolves plugins from marketplaces or local sources, validates manifests, and merges commands, agents, and hooks into the runtime.

Deduplicate conversation branches within the same session. When a session file has multiple leaf messages (from retries or branching), loadAllLogsFromSessionFile produces one LogOption per leaf. Each branch
Standalone bridge daemon. Connects IDE clients and remote-control flows to the same runtime primitives used by the terminal REPL.
AST-based bash command analysis using tree-sitter. This module replaces the shell-quote + hand-rolled char-walker approach in bashSecurity.ts / commands.ts. Instead of detecting parser differentials
Marketplace manager for Claude Code plugins This module provides functionality to: - Manage known marketplace sources (URLs, GitHub repos, npm packages, local files)
Bash-specific safety layer. Checks command rules, speculative classifiers, read-only fast paths, and rich permission metadata before a shell command is allowed to run.
fullyUnquoted before stripSafeRedirections — used by validateBraceExpansion to avoid false negatives from redirection stripping creating backslash adjacencies

Pure-TypeScript port of yoga-layout (Meta's flexbox engine). This matches the `yoga-layout/load` API surface used by src/ink/layout/yoga.ts. The upstream C++ source is ~2500 lines in CalculateLayout.cpp alone; this port
Timeout for individual OAuth requests (metadata discovery, token refresh, etc.)
REPL bridge adapter. Mirrors local conversation state into a bridge session so IDEs and remote surfaces can watch or interact with the same workflow.

most_imported_files.summary: Wrap all CC render calls with ThemeProvider so ThemedBox/ThemedText work without every call site having to mount it. Ink itself is theme-agnostic.

Minimum log level to include in debug output. Defaults to 'debug', which filters out 'verbose' messages. Set CLAUDE_CODE_DEBUG_LOG_LEVEL=verbose to include high-volume diagnostics (e.g. full statusLine command, shell, cwd,

True iff `e` is any of the abort-shaped errors the codebase encounters: our AbortError class, a DOMException from AbortController.abort() (.name === 'AbortError'), or the SDK's APIUserAbortError. The SDK class

Gets the display title for a log/session with fallback logic. Skips firstPrompt if it starts with a tick/goal tag (autonomous mode auto-prompt). Strips display-unfriendly tags (like <ide_opened_file>) from the result.
Check if NODE_OPTIONS contains a specific flag. Splits on whitespace and checks for exact match to avoid false positives.
Core tool protocol definitions: schemas, permission hooks, progress reporting, rendering, and the shared context object passed into every tool.
Threshold in milliseconds for logging slow JSON/clone operations. Operations taking longer than this will be logged for debugging. - Override: set CLAUDE_CODE_SLOW_OPERATION_THRESHOLD_MS to a number
Atomically switch the active session. `sessionId` and `sessionProjectDir` always change together — there is no separate setter for either, so they cannot drift out of sync (CC-34).
Slash-command registry. Loads user-facing commands and uses build-time feature gates to trim command surface by product mode.
Spawn mode for `claude remote-control` multi-session. Set by first-run dialog or `w` toggle.
Analytics service - public API for event logging This module serves as the main entry point for analytics events in Claude CLI. DESIGN: This module has NO dependencies to avoid import cycles.
Subscribe to a slice of AppState. Only re-renders when the selected value changes (compared via Object.is). For multiple independent fields, call the hook multiple times:
User attributes sent to GrowthBook for targeting. Uses UUID suffix (not Uuid) to align with GrowthBook conventions.
Appends a memory correction hint to a rejection/cancellation message when auto-memory is enabled and the GrowthBook flag is on.

Run a function with an overridden working directory for the current async context. All calls to pwd()/getCwd() within the function (and its async descendants) will return the overridden cwd instead of the global one. This enables concurrent
Get the path to the managed settings file based on the current platform
Formats a byte count to a human-readable string (KB, MB, GB). @example formatFileSize(1536) → "1.5KB"
Returns a memoized factory function that constructs the value on first call. Used to defer Zod schema construction from module init time to first access.
Which context this binding belongs to (default: 'Global')

Simplified filesystem operations interface based on Node.js fs module. Provides a subset of commonly used sync operations with type safety. Allows abstraction for alternative implementations (e.g., mock, virtual).
